body_txt: A trademark of most mammals are paws note Paw pads , also affectionately named 'toe beans' by some, are a typical trait of predators who use stealth to catch their prey, as they help silence the sound of their footsteps . They range in size and depend on the breed, but they're usually round, distinct and pointy, and show very well that an animal has been there. They're often treated the same way as fingerprints (which is Truth in Television — pawprints and noseprints are unique to cats and dogs). If the animal has hooves, expect hoofprints. If they're a bird, expect them to use their feet. Basically, this trope is about animals using their anatomy as a way to signify their presence, and to remind the viewer that they're an animal. Consider this the descriptive image form of Furry Reminder . Very common amongst the Funny Animals , even though many of them can actually hold a pen.
